title: 2021-2024 Toyota Sienna Hybrid 12 Volt Vehicle Auxiliary Battery Replacement Instructions
description: Replace the 12V auxiliary battery on 2021-2026 Toyota Sienna hybrid, including bracket removal, vent hose reconnection, and system reinitialization.
tips: Always disconnect the negative terminal first to prevent arcing if the wrench contacts the vehicle body.
Reconnect the positive terminal first before the negative when reinstalling.
Use a vented AGM battery when replacing a hybrid auxiliary battery located inside the vehicle compartment.
Do not overtighten the battery hold-down bracket — overtightening can squeeze and deform the battery case.
Tighten battery bracket and terminal clamps to approximately 5 ft-lbs or until snug.
Ensure the negative terminal clamp is fully seated down on the post before tightening, or it won't grip properly.
Leave sliding doors closed before disconnecting the battery to avoid needing reinitialization, or be prepared to manually close them slowly to initialize.
After reconnection, initialize the power hatch by closing it slowly by hand.
Initialize the backup camera steering lines by turning the steering wheel full lock in each direction if the lines are lost.
An ICS warning light or lane keep assist warning after battery replacement can be cleared by driving straight over 40 mph for approximately 5 seconds.
After reinstallation, inspect vent hose routing to ensure it is not pinched behind the battery.
Apply battery terminal protective spray after reconnection to prevent corrosion.

torque_specs.fastener: Battery hold-down bracket bolt and nut
